input: Transcript snippet (last 40 min of session): Decided to move the auth flow from /api/auth/[...nextauth] to a new custom handler at /api/v2/auth because NextAuth's session callback couldn't be extended to include org-scoped claims without forking. Chose JWT strategy over database sessions to avoid the sessions table migration. Left open: how to handle token refresh for long-lived CLI sessions — no decision made. Edited files: src/lib/auth.ts (new JWT config), src/app/api/v2/auth/route.ts (new handler), middleware.ts (updated matcher). Did NOT update the legacy /api/auth path — it still exists and could cause confusion.
rubric: captures the core architectural decision: moved to /api/v2/auth with custom JWT handler
captures the reason: NextAuth session callback limitation for org-scoped claims
captures the strategy choice: JWT over database sessions and the reason (avoid migration)
lists open question explicitly: token refresh strategy for CLI long-lived sessions
flags critical file paths: src/lib/auth.ts, src/app/api/v2/auth/route.ts, middleware.ts
flags the unresolved risk: legacy /api/auth path still live
output is paste-ready into /clear — self-contained, no implicit context required
captures ≥90% of decisions and open questions from the transcript

input: Transcript snippet: Spent 90 minutes debugging a CLS regression on the product page. Root cause identified: the <Suspense> boundary wrapping the review carousel was resolving after the above-fold content painted, causing a 140px layout shift. Fix attempted: added a min-height placeholder inside Suspense fallback. Fix works on desktop (CLS 0.31 → 0.04) but mobile still shows 0.18 because the min-height is hardcoded in px and the carousel height is dynamic on mobile. Open question: should we use a ResizeObserver to measure the carousel height on first load and store it in localStorage as a hint? Or just use a taller fixed fallback? Decision deferred — needs design input. Relevant files: components/ReviewCarousel.tsx, app/product/[slug]/page.tsx. The Lighthouse test script is at scripts/lighthouse-ci.ts.
rubric: captures root cause: Suspense boundary resolving after paint, causing layout shift
captures fix applied and its scope: min-height placeholder, works on desktop
captures the remaining mobile issue: hardcoded px height vs. dynamic carousel
lists the open question: ResizeObserver + localStorage vs. taller fixed fallback
notes that design input is required before deciding
flags file paths: components/ReviewCarousel.tsx, app/product/[slug]/page.tsx, scripts/lighthouse-ci.ts
output is paste-ready: someone reading it after /clear can continue without re-reading the session
captures ≥90% of decisions and open questions

input: Transcript snippet (database migration planning session): Agreed to use a 3-phase migration for the user_id column: Phase 1 — add new uuid column alongside legacy int id, backfill with a generated UUID for all existing rows. Phase 2 — dual-write period: all new writes go to both columns, reads use int id. Phase 3 — cut over reads to uuid, drop int id. Phase 1 is scripted and tested against a staging snapshot. Phase 2 start date: after the 2.4.0 release, no earlier. Risk flagged: the backfill query on the users table (22M rows) will take ~18 min with current indexing — needs a CONCURRENTLY index before running. Open: who runs the Phase 1 migration in prod? DevOps or engineering lead? Not decided. Migration files: db/migrations/20240312_add_user_uuid.sql, db/migrations/20240313_backfill_uuid.sql. Rollback script: db/rollback/uuid_rollback.sql.
rubric: captures all 3 migration phases and their sequencing
captures the dual-write strategy and read cutover timing
captures the Phase 1 ready status (scripted, tested on staging)
captures Phase 2 gate: after 2.4.0 release
captures the backfill performance risk: ~18 min, 22M rows, needs CONCURRENTLY index
lists the open question: who owns Phase 1 prod execution
flags all file paths: three migration SQL files
captures ≥90% of decisions and open questions

input: Transcript snippet (feature scoping session for a billing overhaul): Three decisions locked: (1) We will support seat-based and usage-based billing simultaneously per org — not one or the other. (2) Stripe will handle all payment processing; we will NOT build our own payment logic. (3) The billing settings page will be gated behind the 'billing_admin' role — regular admins cannot see it. Open issues: (A) How to handle mid-cycle plan changes — proration or credit? No decision. (B) Should the usage metering run as a cron job or event-driven? Leaning event-driven but not finalized. (C) What happens to orgs on the legacy flat-rate plan — forced migration or grandfather? Legal is reviewing. Files touched this session: none yet. Relevant existing files mentioned: src/billing/stripe.ts, src/roles/permissions.ts.
rubric: captures all 3 locked decisions with their exact constraints
captures open question A: proration vs. credit — no decision
captures open question B: cron vs. event-driven — leaning event-driven, not final
captures open question C: legacy plan handling — blocked on legal
notes that no files were edited this session
references existing files: src/billing/stripe.ts, src/roles/permissions.ts
output is paste-ready and clearly delineates locked vs. open items
captures ≥90% of decisions and open questions

input: Transcript snippet (incident post-mortem + follow-up tasking): Incident: at 14:32 UTC the worker queue stopped processing jobs. Root cause: a deploy at 14:28 UTC pushed a config change that set REDIS_MAX_CONNECTIONS=1 (was 50) — a copy-paste error in the .env.production update. Fix: reverted config, redeployed at 15:07 UTC. Queue drained by 15:41 UTC. 69 minutes of job delay; no data loss. Follow-up tasks agreed: (1) Add a config schema validator that rejects REDIS_MAX_CONNECTIONS < 5 at startup (owner: @dmytro, due Friday). (2) Add a Datadog alert for queue depth > 1000 for > 2 min (owner: @priya, due end of sprint). (3) Write a runbook for queue stalls — does not exist (owner: @TBD, not assigned). Open: should we require PR review for .env.production changes? No policy decision made. Relevant files: workers/queue.ts, config/schema.ts (to be created), infra/datadog-alerts.tf.
rubric: captures incident summary: cause, timeline, resolution, impact (69 min delay, no data loss)
captures all 3 follow-up tasks with owners and deadlines where given
flags task 3 as unassigned (TBD)
captures the open policy question: PR review requirement for .env.production
flags config/schema.ts as to-be-created
references existing files: workers/queue.ts, infra/datadog-alerts.tf
output is paste-ready — actionable as a standalone handoff without re-reading the incident thread
captures ≥90% of decisions and open questions
